L. 105–34, § 211(e)(2)(A)“(d)Reporting Requirements.—“(1) .—If there is a distribution to any individual with respect to an interest in a qualified State tuition program during any calendar year, each officer or employee having control of the qualified State tuition program or their designee shall make such reports as the Secretary may require regarding such distribution to the Secretary and to the designated beneficiary or the individual to whom the distribution was made. L. 105–34, title II, § 211(f)111 Stat. 812Aug. 5, 1997“(1).—In generalExcept as otherwise provided in this subsection, the amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 135 and 6693 of this title] shall take effect on .January 1, 1998“(2).—Expenses to include room and boardThe amendment made by subsection (a) shall take effect as if included in the amendments made by section 1806 of the Small Business Job Protection Act of 1996 [].Pub. L. 104–188“(3).—Eligible educational institutionThe amendment made by subsection (b)(2) [amending this section] shall apply to distributions after , with respect to expenses paid after such date (in taxable years ending after such date), for education furnished in academic periods beginning after such date.December 31, 1997“(4).—Coordination with education savings bondsThe amendment made by subsection (c) [amending ] shall apply to taxable years beginning after .section 135 of this titleDecember 31, 1997“(5)Estate and gift tax changes.—“(A).—Gift tax changesParagraphs (2) and (5) of section 529(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended by this section, shall apply to transfers (including designations of new beneficiaries) made after the date of the enactment of this Act [].Aug. 5, 1997“(B).—Estate tax changesParagraph (4) of such section 529(c) shall apply to estates of decedents dying after .June 8, 1997“(6).—Transition rule for pre- contractsaugust 20, 1996In the case of any contract issued prior to , section 529(c)(3)(C) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall be applied for taxable years ending after , without regard to the requirement that a distribution be transferred to a member of the family or the requirement that a change in beneficiaries may be made only to a member of the family.”August 20, 1996August 20, 1996Amendment by section 1601(h)(1)(A), (B) of effective as if included in the provisions of the Small Business Job Protection Act of 1996, , to which it relates, see , set out as a note under .Pub. Effective Date, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–188, title I, § 1806(c)110 Stat. 1898Pub. L. 105–34, title XVI, § 1601(h)(1)(C)111 Stat. 1092Aug. 20, 1996Aug. 5, 1997“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [enacting this section and amending ] shall apply to taxable years ending after the date of the enactment of this Act [].section 135 of this titleAug. 20, 1996“(2).—Transition ruleIf—“(A)a State or agency or instrumentality thereof maintains, on the date of the enactment of this Act, a program under which persons may purchase tuition credits or certificates on behalf of, or make contributions for education expenses of, a designated beneficiary, and“(B)such program meets the requirements of a qualified State tuition program before the later of—“(i)the date which is 1 year after such date of enactment, or“(ii)the first day of the first calendar quarter after the close of the first regular session of the State legislature that begins after such date of enactment,then such program (as in effect on ) shall be treated as a qualified State tuition program with respect to contributions (and earnings allocable thereto) pursuant to contracts entered into under such program before the first date on which such program meets such requirements (determined without regard to this paragraph) and the provisions of such program (as so in effect) shall apply in lieu of section 529(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 with respect to such contributions and earnings.August 20, 1996For purposes of subparagraph (B)(ii), if a State has a 2-year legislative session, each year of such session shall be deemed to be a separate regular session of the State legislature.”

Effective Date, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–188, title I, § 1806(c)110 Stat. 1898Pub. L. 105–34, title XVI, § 1601(h)(1)(C)111 Stat. 1092Aug. 20, 1996Aug. 5, 1997“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [enacting this section and amending ] shall apply to taxable years ending after the date of the enactment of this Act [].section 135 of this titleAug. 20, 1996“(2).—Transition ruleIf—“(A)a State or agency or instrumentality thereof maintains, on the date of the enactment of this Act, a program under which persons may purchase tuition credits or certificates on behalf of, or make contributions for education expenses of, a designated beneficiary, and“(B)such program meets the requirements of a qualified State tuition program before the later of—“(i)the date which is 1 year after such date of enactment, or“(ii)the first day of the first calendar quarter after the close of the first regular session of the State legislature that begins after such date of enactment,then such program (as in effect on ) shall be treated as a qualified State tuition program with respect to contributions (and earnings allocable thereto) pursuant to contracts entered into under such program before the first date on which such program meets such requirements (determined without regard to this paragraph) and the provisions of such program (as so in effect) shall apply in lieu of section 529(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 with respect to such contributions and earnings.August 20, 1996For purposes of subparagraph (B)(ii), if a State has a 2-year legislative session, each year of such session shall be deemed to be a separate regular session of the State legislature.”

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: A qualified tuition program shall be exempt from taxation under this subtitle. Notwithstanding the preceding sentence, such program shall be subject to the taxes imposed by section 511 (relating to imposition of tax on unrelated business income of charitable organizations).

children.label: i
children.text: may purchase tuition credits or certificates on behalf of a designated beneficiary which entitle the beneficiary to the waiver or payment of qualified higher education expenses of the beneficiary, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a program established and maintained by a State or agency or instrumentality thereof, may make contributions to an account which is established for the purpose of meeting the qualified higher education expenses of the designated beneficiary of the account, and

children.label: B
children.text: which meets the other requirements of this subsection.

children.heading: Cash contributions
children.label: 2 Cash contributions
children.text: A program shall not be treated as a qualified tuition program unless it provides that purchases or contributions may only be made in cash.

children.heading: Separate accounting
children.label: 3 Separate accounting
children.text: A program shall not be treated as a qualified tuition program unless it provides separate accounting for each designated beneficiary.

children.heading: Limited investment direction
children.label: 4 Limited investment direction
children.text: A program shall not be treated as a qualified tuition program unless it provides that any contributor to, or designated beneficiary under, such program may, directly or indirectly, direct the investment of any contributions to the program (or any earnings thereon) no more than 2 times in any calendar year.

children.heading: No pledging of interest as security
children.label: 5 No pledging of interest as security
children.text: A program shall not be treated as a qualified tuition program if it allows any interest in the program or any portion thereof to be used as security for a loan.

children.heading: Prohibition on excess contributions
children.label: 6 Prohibition on excess contributions
children.text: A program shall not be treated as a qualified tuition program unless it provides adequate safeguards to prevent contributions on behalf of a designated beneficiary in excess of those necessary to provide for the qualified higher education expenses of the beneficiary.

children.heading: Tax treatment of designated beneficiaries and contributors
children.label: c Tax treatment of designated beneficiaries and contributors
A
children.text: a designated beneficiary under a qualified tuition program, or

children.label: B
children.text: a contributor to such program on behalf of a designated beneficiary,

children.label: i
children.text: shall be treated as a completed gift to such beneficiary which is not a future interest in property, and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall not be treated as a qualified transfer under section 2503(e).

children.heading: Treatment of excess contributions
children.label: B Treatment of excess contributions
children.text: If the aggregate amount of contributions described in subparagraph (A) during the calendar year by a donor exceeds the limitation for such year under section 2503(b), such aggregate amount shall, at the election of the donor, be taken into account for purposes of such section ratably over the 5-year period beginning with such calendar year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any distribution under a qualified tuition program shall be includible in the gross income of the distributee in the manner as provided under section 72 to the extent not excluded from gross income under any other provision of this chapter.

children.heading: Distributions for qualified higher education expenses
children.label: B Distributions for qualified higher education expenses

children.heading: In-kind distributions
children.label: i In-kind distributions
children.text: No amount shall be includible in gross income under subparagraph (A) by reason of a distribution which consists of providing a benefit to the distributee which, if paid for by the distributee, would constitute payment of a qualified higher education expense.

children.label: I
children.text: such distributions do not exceed the qualified higher education expenses (reduced by expenses described in clause (i)), no amount shall be includible in gross income, and

children.label: II
children.text: in any other case, the amount otherwise includible in gross income shall be reduced by an amount which bears the same ratio to such amount as such expenses bear to such distributions.

children.heading: Exception for institutional programs
children.label: iii Exception for institutional programs
children.text: In the case of any taxable year beginning before , clauses (i) and (ii) shall not apply with respect to any distribution during such taxable year under a qualified tuition program established and maintained by 1 or more eligible educational institutions.January 1, 2004

children.heading: Treatment as distributions
children.label: iv Treatment as distributions
children.text: Any benefit furnished to a designated beneficiary under a qualified tuition program shall be treated as a distribution to the beneficiary for purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Coordination with American Opportunity and Lifetime Learning credits
children.label: v Coordination with American Opportunity and Lifetime Learning credits
I
children.text: as provided in section 25A(g)(2), and

children.label: II
children.text: by the amount of such expenses which were taken into account in determining the credit allowed to the taxpayer or any other person under section 25A.

children.heading: Coordination with Coverdell education savings accounts
children.label: vi Coordination with Coverdell education savings accounts
I
children.text: the aggregate distributions to which clauses (i) and (ii) and section 530(d)(2)(A) apply, exceed

children.label: II
children.text: the total amount of qualified higher education expenses otherwise taken into account under clauses (i) and (ii) (after the application of clause (v)) for such year,

children.heading: Change in beneficiaries or programs
children.label: C Change in beneficiaries or programs
I
children.text: to another qualified tuition program for the benefit of the designated beneficiary,

children.label: II
children.text: to the credit of another designated beneficiary under a qualified tuition program who is a member of the family of the designated beneficiary with respect to which the distribution was made, or

children.label: III
children.text: to an ABLE account (as defined in section 529A(e)(6)) of the designated beneficiary or a member of the family of the designated beneficiary.

children.heading: Change in designated beneficiaries
children.label: ii Change in designated beneficiaries
children.text: Any change in the designated beneficiary of an interest in a qualified tuition program shall not be treated as a distribution for purposes of subparagraph (A) if the new beneficiary is a member of the family of the old beneficiary.

children.heading: Limitation on certain rollovers
children.label: iii Limitation on certain rollovers
children.text: Clause (i)(I) shall not apply to any transfer if such transfer occurs within 12 months from the date of a previous transfer to any qualified tuition program for the benefit of the designated beneficiary.

children.heading: Special rule for contributions of refunded amounts
children.label: D Special rule for contributions of refunded amounts
children.text: In the case of a beneficiary who receives a refund of any qualified higher education expenses from an eligible educational institution, subparagraph (A) shall not apply to that portion of any distribution for the taxable year which is recontributed to a qualified tuition program of which such individual is a beneficiary, but only to the extent such recontribution is made not later than 60 days after the date of such refund and does not exceed the refunded amount.

children.heading: Special rollover to roth iras from long-term qualified tuition programs
children.label: E Special rollover to roth iras from long-term qualified tuition programs
I
children.text: does not exceed the aggregate amount contributed to the program (and earnings attributable thereto) before the 5-year period ending on the date of the distribution, and

children.label: II
children.text: is paid in a direct trustee-to-trustee transfer to a Roth IRA maintained for the benefit of such designated beneficiary.

children.heading: Annual limitation
children.label: I Annual limitation
children.text: Clause (i) shall only apply to so much of any distribution as does not exceed the amount applicable to the designated beneficiary under section 408A(c)(2) for the taxable year (reduced by the amount of aggregate contributions made during the taxable year to all individual retirement plans maintained for the benefit of the designated beneficiary).

children.heading: Aggregate limitation
children.label: II Aggregate limitation
children.text: This subparagraph shall not apply to any distribution described in clause (i) to the extent that the aggregate amount of such distributions with respect to the designated beneficiary for such taxable year and all prior taxable years exceeds $35,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No amount shall be includible in the gross estate of any individual for purposes of chapter 11 by reason of an interest in a qualified tuition program.

children.heading: Amounts includible in estate of designated beneficiary in certain cases
children.label: B Amounts includible in estate of designated beneficiary in certain cases
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to amounts distributed on account of the death of a beneficiary.

children.heading: Amounts includible in estate of donor making excess contributions
children.label: C Amounts includible in estate of donor making excess contributions
children.text: In the case of a donor who makes the election described in paragraph (2)(B) and who dies before the close of the 5-year period referred to in such paragraph, notwithstanding subparagraph (A), the gross estate of the donor shall include the portion of such contributions properly allocable to periods after the date of death of the donor.

children.heading: Treatment of distributions
children.label: A Treatment of distributions
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), in no event shall a distribution from a qualified tuition program be treated as a taxable gift.

children.heading: Treatment of designation of new beneficiary
children.label: B Treatment of designation of new beneficiary
i
children.text: assigned to the same generation as (or a higher generation than) the old beneficiary (determined in accordance with section 2651), and

children.label: ii
children.text: a member of the family of the old beneficiary.

children.heading: Additional tax
children.label: 6 Additional tax
children.text: The tax imposed by section 530(d)(4) shall apply to any payment or distribution from a qualified tuition program in the same manner as such tax applies to a payment or distribution from a Coverdell education savings account. This paragraph shall not apply to any payment or distribution in any taxable year beginning before , which is includible in gross income but used for qualified higher education expenses of the designated beneficiary.January 1, 2004

children.heading: Treatment of elementary and secondary tuition
children.label: 7 Treatment of elementary and secondary tuition
A
children.text: Tuition.

children.label: B
children.text: Curriculum and curricular materials.

children.label: C
children.text: Books or other instructional materials.

children.label: D
children.text: Online educational materials.

children.label: i
children.text: is licensed as a teacher in any State,

children.label: ii
children.text: has taught at an eligible educational institution, or

children.label: iii
children.text: is a subject matter expert in the relevant subject.

children.label: F
children.text: Fees for a nationally standardized norm-referenced achievement test, an advanced placement examination, or any examinations related to college or university admission.

children.label: G
children.text: Fees for dual enrollment in an institution of higher education.

children.label: H
children.text: Educational therapies for students with disabilities provided by a licensed or accredited practitioner or provider, including occupational, behavioral, physical, and speech-language therapies.

children.heading: Treatment of certain expenses associated with registered apprenticeship programs
children.label: 8 Treatment of certain expenses associated with registered apprenticeship programs
children.text: Any reference in this subsection to the term “qualified higher education expense” shall include a reference to expenses for fees, books, supplies, and equipment required for the participation of a designated beneficiary in an apprenticeship program registered and certified with the Secretary of Labor under section 1 of the National Apprenticeship Act ().29 U.S.C. 50

children.heading: Treatment of qualified education loan repayments
children.label: 9 Treatment of qualified education loan repayments

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any reference in this subsection to the term “qualified higher education expense” shall include a reference to amounts paid as principal or interest on any qualified education loan (as defined in section 221(d)) of the designated beneficiary or a sibling of the designated beneficiary.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The amount of distributions treated as a qualified higher education expense under this paragraph with respect to the loans of any individual shall not exceed $10,000 (reduced by the amount of distributions so treated for all prior taxable years).

children.heading: Special rules for siblings of the designated beneficiary
children.label: C Special rules for siblings of the designated beneficiary

children.heading: Separate accounting
children.label: i Separate accounting
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B) and subsection (d), amounts treated as a qualified higher education expense with respect to the loans of a sibling of the designated beneficiary shall be taken into account with respect to such sibling and not with respect to such designated beneficiary.

children.heading: Sibling defined
children.label: ii Sibling defined
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “sibling” means an individual who bears a relationship to the designated beneficiary which is described in section 152(d)(2)(B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each officer or employee having control of the qualified tuition program or their designee shall make such reports regarding such program to the Secretary and to designated beneficiaries with respect to contributions, distributions, and such other matters as the Secretary may require. The reports required by this paragraph shall be filed at such time and in such manner and furnished to such individuals at such time and in such manner as may be required by the Secretary.

children.heading: Rollover distributions
children.label: 2 Rollover distributions
children.text: In the case of any distribution described in subsection (c)(3)(E), the officer or employee having control of the qualified tuition program (or their designee) shall provide a report to the trustee of the Roth IRA to which the distribution is made. Such report shall be filed at such time and in such manner as the Secretary may require and shall include information with respect to the contributions, distributions, and earnings of the qualified tuition program as of the date of the distribution described in subsection (c)(3)(A), together with such other matters as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Other definitions and special rules
children.label: e Other definitions and special rules
A
children.text: the individual designated at the commencement of participation in the qualified tuition program as the beneficiary of amounts paid (or to be paid) to the program,

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a change in beneficiaries described in subsection (c)(3)(C), the individual who is the new beneficiary, and

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of an interest in a qualified tuition program purchased by a State or local government (or agency or instrumentality thereof) or an organization described in section 501(c)(3) and exempt from taxation under section 501(a) as part of a scholarship program operated by such government or organization, the individual receiving such interest as a scholarship.

children.label: A
children.text: the spouse of such beneficiary;

children.label: B
children.text: an individual who bears a relationship to such beneficiary which is described in subparagraphs (A) through (G) of section 152(d)(2);

children.label: C
children.text: the spouse of any individual described in subparagraph (B); and

children.label: D
children.text: any first cousin of such beneficiary.

children.label: i
children.text: tuition, fees, books, supplies, and equipment required for the enrollment or attendance of a designated beneficiary at an eligible educational institution,

children.label: ii
children.text: expenses for special needs services in the case of a special needs beneficiary which are incurred in connection with such enrollment or attendance, and

children.label: iii
children.text: expenses for the purchase of computer or peripheral equipment (as defined in section 168(i)(2)(B)), computer software (as defined in section 197(e)(3)(B)), or Internet access and related services, if such equipment, software, or services are to be used primarily by the beneficiary during any of the years the beneficiary is enrolled at an eligible educational institution.

children.heading: Room and board included for students who are at least half-time
children.label: B Room and board included for students who are at least half-time

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of an individual who is an eligible student (as defined in section 25A(b)(3)) for any academic period, such term shall also include reasonable costs for such period (as determined under the qualified tuition program) incurred by the designated beneficiary for room and board while attending such institution. For purposes of subsection (b)(6), a designated beneficiary shall be treated as meeting the requirements of this clause.

children.label: I
children.text: the allowance (applicable to the student) for room and board included in the cost of attendance (as defined in section 472 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (), as in effect on the date of the enactment of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001) as determined by the eligible educational institution for such period, or20 U.S.C. 1087ll

children.label: II
children.text: if greater, the actual invoice amount the student residing in housing owned or operated by the eligible educational institution is charged by such institution for room and board costs for such period.

children.heading: Certain postsecondary credentialing expenses
children.label: C Certain postsecondary credentialing expenses
children.text: The term “qualified higher education expenses” includes qualified postsecondary credentialing expenses (as defined in subsection (f)).

children.heading: Application of section 514
children.label: 4 Application of section 514
children.text: An interest in a qualified tuition program shall not be treated as debt for purposes of section 514.

children.label: A
children.text: which is described in section 481 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (), as in effect on the date of the enactment of this paragraph, and20 U.S.C. 1088

children.label: B
children.text: which is eligible to participate in a program under title IV of such Act.

children.label: A
children.text: tuition, fees, books, supplies, and equipment required for the enrollment or attendance of a designated beneficiary in a recognized postsecondary credential program, or any other expense incurred in connection with enrollment in or attendance at a recognized postsecondary credential program if such expense would, if incurred in connection with enrollment or attendance at an eligible educational institution, be covered under subsection (e)(3)(A),

children.label: B
children.text: fees for testing if such testing is required to obtain or maintain a recognized postsecondary credential, and

children.label: C
children.text: fees for continuing education if such education is required to maintain a recognized postsecondary credential.

children.label: A
children.text: such program is included on a State list prepared under section 122(d) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (),29 U.S.C. 3152(d)

children.label: B
children.text: such program is listed in the public directory of the Web Enabled Approval Management System (WEAMS) of the Veterans Benefits Administration, or successor directory such program,

children.label: C
children.text: an examination (developed or administered by an organization widely recognized as providing reputable credentials in the occupation) is required to obtain or maintain such credential and such organization recognizes such program as providing training or education which prepares individuals to take such examination, or

children.label: D
children.text: such program is identified by the Secretary, after consultation with the Secretary of Labor, as being a reputable program for obtaining a recognized postsecondary credential for purposes of this subparagraph.

children.label: i
children.text: any postsecondary employment credential issued by a program that is accredited by the Institute for Credentialing Excellence, the National Commission on Certifying Agencies, or the American National Standards Institute,

children.label: ii
children.text: any postsecondary employment credential that is included in the Credentialing Opportunities On-Line (COOL) directory of credentialing programs (or successor directory) maintained by the Department of Defense or by any branch of the Armed Forces, or

children.label: iii
children.text: any postsecondary employment credential identified for purposes of this clause by the Secretary, after consultation with the Secretary of Labor, as being industry recognized,

children.label: B
children.text: any certificate of completion of an apprenticeship that is registered and certified with the Secretary of Labor under the Act of (commonly known as the “National Apprenticeship Act”; , chapter 663; et seq.),August 16, 193750 Stat. 66429 U.S.C. 50

children.label: C
children.text: any occupational or professional license issued or recognized by a State or the Federal Government (and any certification that satisfies a condition for obtaining such a license), and

children.label: D
children.text: any recognized postsecondary credential as defined in section 3(52) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (), provided through a program described in paragraph (2)(A).29 U.S.C. 3102(52)

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: g Regulations
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, the Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section and to prevent abuse of such purposes, including regulations under chapters 11, 12, and 13 of this title.
